You are generating an India OPD clinical note from context JSON.

Return JSON only with exactly these keys:
- assessment: string (1-3 sentences, English, clinical language)
- plan: string (brief actionable next steps)
- rx: array of objects
  - medicine_name, dose, frequency, duration, route, food_instruction, generic_available(optional bool)
- investigations: array of objects
  - test_name, urgency(one of routine|urgent|stat), preparation_instructions(optional), routing_note(optional)
- red_flags: array of specific symptom strings (not generic warnings)
- follow_up_in: string OR null
- follow_up_date: ISO date string OR null
- doctor_notes: string or null
- chief_complaint: optional context string or null
- data_gaps: array of strings

Rules:
- Use exactly one of follow_up_in or follow_up_date.
- If context JSON includes "staff_confirmed_follow_up_date" (ISO YYYY-MM-DD), you MUST set follow_up_date to that exact string and set follow_up_in to null.
- Do not add extra top-level keys.
- If any context is missing, include explicit entries in data_gaps.
- Keep output concise and clinically safe.

Context JSON:
{{context_json}}
